summary: Zhou Yu; takes Nanjun in a costly battle - shot by an arrow during the siege and lures Cao Ren out with a feigned-dead ploy. Comes to the wall to find Zhuge Liang's banner already flying. Liu Bei has slipped Zhao Yun in to take the city while Zhou Yu fought; Zhou Yu coughs blood at the sight, opening the wound.
Cao Ren; defends Nanjun against Zhou Yu; falls for the feigned-dead trap and sallies out to capture the body, only to be ambushed. Withdraws to Xiangyang with the remnant.
Liu Bei; pre-positions Zhao Yun, Guan Yu, and Zhang Fei to seize Nanjun, Jingzhou, and Xiangyang the moment Zhou Yu's army weakens Cao Ren. By chapter's end he holds all three on Zhuge Liang's plan.
Zhao Yun; slips into Nanjun while Zhou Yu is wounded; raises Liu Bei's banner over the city.
Guan Yu; takes Xiangyang by Zhuge Liang's stratagem using a forged Cao Ren letter to draw out Xiahou Dun's nominal force.
Zhang Fei; takes Jingzhou City using a forged Cao Ren letter to draw out the defenders.
Zhuge Liang; engineers the three-city seizure during Zhou Yu's siege. Zhou Yu's first great humiliation at his hands.
Lu Su; sent by Zhou Yu to demand Jingzhou back from Liu Bei; talked into a 'borrowing' arrangement by Zhuge Liang.
Cao Cao; orders the Nanjun defence and the relief from Xuchang - too late to save the three cities. Yanzhou + the north consolidated; the south now a frontier.
Cao Hong; commands part of Cao Ren's defence.
Cao Hong (duplicate record).
Cao Chun; Cao Cao's relative; commands part of the Cao Ren forces.
Xiahou Dun; commands Xiangyang's nominal defence; falls for the forged-letter ruse.
Niu Jin; Cao Ren officer; killed in the Nanjun fighting.
Chen Jiao; senior Wei civil officer named in passing.
Chen Jiao (duplicate record).
Cheng Pu; deputy to Zhou Yu in the Nanjun siege.
Han Dang; with Zhou Yu at Nanjun.
Gan Ning; with Zhou Yu at Nanjun.
Jiang Qin; with Zhou Yu at Nanjun.
Pan Zhang; with Zhou Yu at Nanjun.
Chen Wu; with Zhou Yu at Nanjun.
Xu Sheng; with Zhou Yu at Nanjun.
Ding Feng; with Zhou Yu at Nanjun.
Ding Feng (duplicate record).
Zhou Tai; with Zhou Yu at Nanjun.
Zhou Tai (duplicate record).
Sun Quan; coordinates the southern campaign from Chaisang; begins his own push at Hefei (the parallel front).
Liu Biao; named in passing - long dead.
Sun Qian; with Liu Bei.
Sun Qian (duplicate record).
Liang Da; minor figure named in passing.
Shentu Pan; minor figure named in passing.

Liu Bei; accepts Liu Qi's death and his unofficial succession to Jingzhou by the merit of having protected him to the end. Then accepts Lu Su's renegotiated 'borrowing' deal - Liu Bei holds Jingzhou as Liu Qi's regent. Zhao Yun is dispatched against Zhao Fan at Guiyang.

Zhao Yun; takes Guiyang from Zhao Fan with one battle. Politely refuses Zhao Fan's offer of his widowed sister-in-law's hand in marriage - the famous 'too irregular and unworthy' refusal that becomes Liu Bei's lesson in Zhao Yun's character.

Zhao Fan; Governor of Guiyang; submits to Zhao Yun after a brief fight and tries to bind him by family by offering his widowed sister-in-law Lady Fan as wife. Zhao Yun politely declines; Zhao Fan rebels later and is killed.
Liu Qi; dies in this chapter - Zhuge Liang foresees it from a falling star. Liu Bei mourns him sincerely, then takes nominal possession of Jingzhou as Liu Qi's heir.
Liu Du; Governor of Lingling; one of the four southern commanderies - surrenders to Liu Bei via Yi Ji.
Liu Xiang; Liu Du's son; named in passing in the surrender.
Chen Ying; minor figure named in passing during the Guiyang campaign.
Ma Liang; arrives at Liu Bei's court at chapter's end as a senior counsel - the famous 'White Eyebrow' eldest of the five Ma brothers.
Ma Su; Ma Liang's younger brother; recommended into Liu Bei's circle. Will be the disastrous Ma Su of Jieting in chapter 95.
Yi Ji; sent by Liu Bei as envoy to Liu Du at Lingling - secures the bloodless surrender.
Mi Zhu; with Liu Bei at Jingzhou.
Zhuge Liang; calibrates the four-commandery campaign - Zhao Yun for Guiyang, Zhang Fei for Wuling, Guan Yu for Changsha. All accomplished in one chapter.
Guan Yu; volunteers for the Changsha campaign; sets out at chapter's end.
Zhang Fei; takes Wuling from Jin Xuan with a single charge.
Liu Feng; with Liu Bei at Jingzhou.
Lu Su; comes to Liu Bei's court to renegotiate the Jingzhou borrowing arrangement after Liu Qi's death.
Zhou Yu; at Chaisang; receives news of the four-commandery sweep and seethes with the second great humiliation.
Cheng Pu; with Zhou Yu.
Sun Quan; at Chaisang.
Cao Cao; named in passing - consolidating the north after Red Cliffs.
Liu Biao; named in passing - long dead.
Shentu Pan; minor figure named in passing.

Guan Yu at Changsha - the classic Huang Zhong duel. After three days of single-combat with the aged general (each unable to defeat the other; mutual respect builds), Guan Yu refuses to shoot Huang Zhong from behind when the latter's horse falls. The next day Huang Zhong's bow returns the favour - sends the arrow into Guan Yu's helmet plume rather than into his heart. The mutual mercy that becomes legend.

Huang Zhong's defining chapter - about 60 years old; defends Changsha against Guan Yu. The horse-fall mercy and the deliberate plume-shot establish his code and Guan Yu's. Han Xuan accuses him of treason and orders his execution; rescued by Wei Yan's mutiny; defects to Liu Bei.

Wei Yan; the famous mutiny - kills Han Xuan with one stroke to save Huang Zhong, opens Changsha's gates to Guan Yu. Joins Liu Bei. Zhuge Liang spots 'a rebellious bone at the back of his skull' and almost has him executed; Liu Bei intervenes. The seed of Wei Yan's controversial arc.
Wei Yan (duplicate record).
Han Xuan; Governor of Changsha; orders Huang Zhong's execution for not killing Guan Yu; killed by Wei Yan's mutiny.
Han Xuan (duplicate record).
Liu Bei; visits Huang Zhong's house in person to persuade him into service after the surrender. Receives Wei Yan into the army over Zhuge Liang's objection.

Zhuge Liang; spots the 'rebellious bone' at the back of Wei Yan's neck and recommends his immediate execution. Liu Bei overrules him - a rare disagreement between them. Sets up Wei Yan's eventual fate.
Zhang Fei; finishes the Wuling settlement.
Zhao Yun; finishes the Guiyang settlement.
Cao Cao; in this chapter dispatches Zhang Liao to Hefei to face Sun Quan's northern push - opens the second great front.
Zhang Liao; sent to Hefei by Cao Cao to face Sun Quan; arrives just before Sun Quan's army does. Will become the hero of Hefei in chapter 67.
Sun Quan; marches against Hefei with 100,000 troops; the parallel southern campaign.
Cheng Pu; with Sun Quan.
Taishi Ci; with Sun Quan at Hefei - first action.
Gan Ning; with Sun Quan at Hefei.
Li Dian; with Zhang Liao at Hefei.
Liu Pan; Liu Biao's old commander; named in passing in the Changsha campaign.
Liu Qi; named in passing - dead.
Liu Biao; named in passing - dead.
Jin Xuan; Governor of Wuling; killed by Zhang Fei's army.
Dong Xi; with Sun Quan.
Song Qian; named in passing.
Lu Xun; with Sun Quan.
Lu Su; with Sun Quan.
Zhou Yu; with Sun Quan.
Zhang Zhao; with Sun Quan.
Zhang Zhao (duplicate record).
Zhang Hong; with Sun Quan.
Zhang Hong (duplicate record).
Zhang Hong (duplicate record).
Zhuge Jin; with Sun Quan.
Sun He; minor figure named in passing.
Xia Yu; minor figure named in passing.
Yang Lin; minor figure named in passing.
Shentu Pan; minor figure named in passing.

Sun Quan; on Zhou Yu's advice offers Liu Bei a marriage alliance with his younger sister Lady Sun - meant as the trap that will let Wu seize Liu Bei or extort Jingzhou back. Falls into Zhuge Liang's counter-scheme.
Zhou Yu; designs the marriage-trap scheme; Lady Sun is the bait. Wu will hold Liu Bei in Wu while Zhou Yu retakes Jingzhou - except Zhuge Liang knew it ahead of time.
Liu Bei; persuaded by Zhuge Liang's three silken bag instructions to accept the marriage; sets out across the river with Zhao Yun's escort. The chapter ends with him meeting his bride.

Zhuge Liang; gives Zhao Yun three sealed silken bags of instructions to open in sequence during the Wu visit; the first orders the public engagement parade that locks Sun Quan's mother into approving the marriage.

Zhao Yun; escorts Liu Bei to Wu with the three silken bags. Executes the first bag's instructions - parades the engagement so publicly through Nanxu that Lady Wu (Sun Quan's mother) hears of it before Sun Quan can quietly cancel.
Lu Su; carries the marriage proposal.
Lu Fan; Wu envoy who proposes the marriage.
Lu Fan (duplicate record).
Cao Cao; named in passing - far in the north.
Liu Biao; named in passing - dead.
Liu Qi; named in passing - dead.
Liu Zhang; named in passing in the regional roll.
Lady Gan; named in passing - Liu Bei's existing senior wife at Jingzhou.
Liu Shan; named in passing - at Jingzhou.
Sun Qian; with Liu Bei.
Sun Qian (duplicate record).
Sun Ce; named in passing.
Shentu Pan; minor figure named in passing.

Liu Bei; opens Zhuge Liang's second silken bag - fakes Cao Cao's southern invasion to convince Sun Quan to let him return to Jingzhou. Lady Sun rides at his side. They escape across the river under Zhou Yu's pursuit; Lady Sun's prestige cows the pursuers at every river crossing.

Lady Sun; the famous 'turning her chariot upon her own pursuers' chapter. Rides at Liu Bei's side and drives off Zhou Yu's intercepting officers with her own voice and sword - the Sun-family majesty made manifest.
Lady Sun (duplicate record).
Zhao Yun; escorts Liu Bei and Lady Sun all the way back; opens the second silken bag at the right moment.

Zhou Yu; his marriage-trap scheme fails; he sends Jiang Qin and Pan Zhang after the fleeing couple. Both are turned back by Lady Sun's voice. Zhou Yu reads Zhuge Liang's note 'Zhou Yu gave away a bride and lost an army' and coughs blood. Second great humiliation.
Jiang Qin; sent by Zhou Yu to intercept Liu Bei and Lady Sun; turned back by Lady Sun's reprimand.
Pan Zhang; sent by Zhou Yu to intercept; turned back by Lady Sun.
Han Dang; sent in the pursuit.
Zhou Tai; sent in the pursuit.
Zhou Tai (duplicate record).
Chen Wu; sent in the pursuit.
Ding Feng; with Zhou Yu in the pursuit.
Ding Feng (duplicate record).
Xu Sheng; with Zhou Yu in the pursuit.
Cheng Pu; with Zhou Yu.
Huang Gai; with Zhou Yu.
Huang Gai (duplicate record).
Sun Quan; deceived by Zhou Yu's plan and his own mother's affection for Liu Bei; lets the couple slip away. Then learns of Zhou Yu's defeat.

Zhuge Liang; arranges Guan Yu and Huang Zhong and Wei Yan in three covering ambushes that finish Zhou Yu's pursuers - the third silken bag. Sends Zhou Yu the famous taunting verses that bring on his collapse.
Huang Zhong; commands one of the three ambushes that cover Liu Bei's return.
Guan Yu; commands one of the three ambushes.
Wei Yan; commands one of the three ambushes.
Wei Yan (duplicate record).
Sun Qian; with Liu Bei.
Sun Qian (duplicate record).
Liang Da; minor figure named in passing.
Cao Cao; named in passing.
Shentu Pan; minor figure named in passing.
Zhang Zhao; with Sun Quan.
Zhang Zhao (duplicate record).
